query: Florida 2026 gubernatorial GOP primary debate schedule Byron Donalds James Fishback David Jolly - any debates scheduled or held summer 2026
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings:

## Summary: Florida 2026 Gubernatorial GOP Primary Debate Schedule

**One debate held (summer 2026):** A GOP gubernatorial debate took place **Thursday, July 2–3, 2026** as an **online/YouTube event** ("The Future of Florida" live debate), hosted and moderated by podcaster **Patrick Bet-David (PBD Podcast)** after no TV network would air it. Three of four major candidates participated — **Paul Renner, Jay Collins, and James Fishback** — while frontrunner **Byron Donalds skipped it** (though he was named 60+ times). Collins and Fishback clashed heatedly, partly over Collins's lawsuit to disqualify Fishback on residency grounds.

**No official party debate:** The **Republican Party of Florida** set qualification thresholds that **only Donalds met** (leading polls ~40–60%, raising $80M+, Trump-endorsed). Because rivals didn't qualify and Donalds refused to debate ("It's not my job to help them," July 6), **no RPOF-sanctioned primary debate occurred**. The rules drew backlash, including from Gov. DeSantis.

**Important clarification:** **David Jolly is the *Democratic* frontrunner**, not a GOP candidate — he was never in any GOP primary debate.

**Only scheduled debates:** General-election (post-primary) debates at Florida Atlantic University via "Decision 2026" — **Senate Oct. 13** and **Governor Oct. 15, 2026** — but the RPOF says it hasn't sanctioned these, and both the RPOF and Jolly called the announcement premature.

**Bottom line:** Exactly one GOP primary-season debate happened in summer 2026 (the unofficial July online event Donalds skipped); no official party primary debate was held or is scheduled.

sources.snippet: David Jolly (Democrat) leads Democratic primary by 15 points; Donalds leads GOP primary by 39 points (Change Research, May 2026).

key_facts: A GOP gubernatorial debate was held Thursday, July 2–3, 2026 — an online/YouTube event ('The Future of Florida' live debate) hosted and moderated by podcaster Patrick Bet-David (PBD Podcast).
Participants were Paul Renner, Jay Collins, and James Fishback; Byron Donalds skipped it but was named 60+ times during the ~two-hour event.
No broadcast or cable network agreed to air the July debate, so it ran only online.
Byron Donalds was the ONLY candidate to meet the Republican Party of Florida's polling/fundraising/organizational qualification thresholds, so no official RPOF-sanctioned primary debate was held.
Donalds publicly refused to debate (July 6, 2026), saying his opponents can't win statewide: 'It's not my job to help them.'
Collins, Fishback, and Renner all publicly demanded debates; the RPOF debate rules drew backlash, including criticism from Gov. DeSantis.
Jay Collins filed a lawsuit (~June 29, 2026) to disqualify James Fishback from the ballot over Florida residency/eligibility, which fueled clashes at the July debate.
David Jolly is the DEMOCRATIC frontrunner, not a GOP candidate — he was not part of any GOP primary debate.

General-election (not primary) debates were announced by the nonpartisan 'Decision 2026: Before YOU Vote' series at Florida Atlantic University: U.S. Senate on Oct. 13, 2026 and Governor on Oct. 15, 2026 (7–8 p.m. ET).
The Republican Party of Florida said it has NOT sanctioned any statewide debates; both RPOF Chair Evan Power and David Jolly called the October debate announcement premature.
Florida primaries are Aug. 18, 2026; general election Nov. 3, 2026; Donalds is the Trump-endorsed frontrunner (~39-point primary lead).
